findings.evidence: `rg` hits: `@[expose] noncomputable def cm_density` at line 69, `cm_measure` at line 93, `bernstein_kernel` at line 243, and `cm_rescaled` at line 286; the rubric permits exposed bodies only when consumers must unfold or compute, which these measure/kernel abstractions do not require.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from these definitions and keep downstream use routed through the public characteristic lemmas; if a lemma currently relies on `rfl`, prove it in the same module without making the body unfoldable to importers, or add the missing local/private helper lemma instead.
findings.issue: `cm_density`, `cm_measure`, `bernstein_kernel`, and `cm_rescaled` are still marked `@[expose]`, so their bodies are part of the public interface despite having wrapper lemmas.

summary: The added characteristic lemmas cover the earlier missing normal forms, but the public definitions still expose their implementation bodies. That leaves downstream code able to depend directly on the chosen `if`/`withDensity`/`map` encodings instead of the intended API.
